patterns.name: PostgreSQL Connection String with Password
patterns.description: PostgreSQL connection URI containing username and password
patterns.risk: Direct access to the database. Attacker can read, modify, or delete all data.
patterns.fix: Use DATABASE_URL environment variable. Use connection pooler (PgBouncer) with SSL.
false_positive_hints: postgres://user:password@localhost/ in docs is usually an example

patterns.name: MySQL Connection String with Password
patterns.description: MySQL connection URI containing username and password
patterns.risk: Direct database access with embedded credentials.
patterns.fix: Use DATABASE_URL or MYSQL_PASSWORD environment variables.

patterns.name: MongoDB Connection String with Password
patterns.risk: Direct access to MongoDB. Can read/write all collections in the database.
patterns.fix: Use MONGODB_URI environment variable. Enable authentication and TLS.

patterns.name: Redis Connection with Password
patterns.description: Redis connection URI with embedded password
patterns.risk: Access to Redis. Can read cached data, session tokens, and queue messages.

patterns.name: Supabase Service Role Key
patterns.description: Supabase service role key (bypasses Row Level Security)
patterns.risk: CRITICAL: Bypasses all RLS policies. Full read/write access to all tables.
patterns.fix: Use SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY env var. NEVER expose in client-side code.
